message.content:
Test file: src/claim-office.spec.ts
Implementation file: src/claim-office.ts
Failing test: "three alike components form a building block (3 runes → 65 G)"
Current error: Expected { premium: 65 }, received { premium: 80 }
Passing tests so far: 7

Spec rule: Components (e.g., runes/moonstones) cost 25 G each. EXACTLY 3 alike components form a "building block" with a special base premium of 60 G. So 3 runes = 60 G + 5 fee = 65 G.

Note: Future tests will require 4 runes = 100+5 = 105 (no block) and 2 runes + 1 moonstone = 75+5 = 80 (different types, no block). For now, focus only on the 3-alike case.

Implement minimal change to make this pass while keeping prior tests passing.

Component types include "rune" and likely "moonstone". You can identify components as types with base premium 25 (or specifically rune/moonstone).

Do NOT refactor. EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously, return after completion.

content.text: I'll analyze the current implementation and test file, then make a minimal change to pass the failing test.

Now I understand. Currently 3 runes give 25*3+5 = 80. I need to make it 60+5 = 65 when there are exactly 3 alike components.

Minimal change: detect when items are exactly 3 of the same component type (rune) and return 60 as the base instead of 75.

content.text:
All 8 tests pass.

Green Phase Complete:
**Implementation**: Added a small helper `isThreeAlikeBlock` that detects when items are exactly 3 of the same component type (base premium 25). When detected, `quote` uses a base of 60 G instead of summing individual premiums. Added the processing fee on top in both cases.
**Result**: All tests now pass (8 passing, 16 todo)
**Approach**: Minimal conditional branch. The check is intentionally narrow (length === 3, all same type, component-typed via base premium 25) so future tests for "4 runes" and "2 runes + 1 moonstone" will naturally fall through to the normal sum path. No refactoring performed.
